title: Geophysical Surveying and Mapping Services

narrative.executive_view: Geophysical surveying and mapping has meaningful AI leverage in sensor processing, classification and reporting, but field acquisition, client interpretation and regulated/boundary-adjacent work retain human value. [S1][S2]
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Lidar, remote sensing and geophysical data create large processing workloads suited to AI; collection design, field operations and accountable interpretation remain human. [S1][S3]
narrative.value_capture: Specialized sensors, data rights and client workflows retain some savings, but analytics tools diffuse and clients may demand faster, cheaper deliverables.
narrative.adoption_timing: AI is increasingly embedded in data processing, but effective operations require validated sensor data and client acceptance; current effective adoption is below 50%.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The supplied target count is small. No direct size-tier M&A evidence was reopened, so multiples are conservative estimates.
narrative.terminal_demand: Demand is durable for physical data collection and specialist interpretation, though AI reduces routine processing labor. [S1]
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The 5413 role mix is engineering-contaminated; energy/minerals/environmental subsegments, project cycles and sensor capital intensity make this a low-confidence aggregation.

sources.title: USGS 3D Elevation Program
sources.what_it_supports: USGS says 99% of the nation had baseline 3DEP data available or in progress at FY2025 end; lidar supports private mapping firms.

sources.title: 2026 ALTA/NSPS Standards
sources.what_it_supports: ALTA/NSPS surveys are boundary surveys with state-law and field/perimeter requirements.

sources.title: New Mexico surveying definitions
sources.what_it_supports: Boundary surveying includes legal/field evidence; lidar/UAS are measurement technologies.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541360 ($1,461,125,114) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541360 ($1,992,974,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $274,931 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541360); class <5: 726 firms, avg revenue/firm $284,807 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $44,572 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 99 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,215,010 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $190,149 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 65 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,396,492 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $375,051 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 45 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,642,467 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,039,546 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $39,378,118 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,162,675 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $33,862,938 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,299,550 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 78
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Processing exposure is meaningful but field validation slows effective adoption.

terminal_value.justification: Physical acquisition and specialized interpretation persist despite automated mapping. [S1]

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No; role mix is parent 5413 proxy.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Faster data processing pressures deliverable pricing while field deployment remains scarce.

sensitivity: Subsegment and field-labor share change V.

reviewer_note: All score-driving sources were reopened; direct small-firm multiple evidence was unavailable.
